questions.subject: History
questions.prompt:
With reference to the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA), consider the following statements:
1. It is an autonomous institute functioning under the Ministry of Culture.
2. It was established in 2014 specifically to serve as the Nodal Centre for India’s engagement with the UNESCO Memory of the World programme.
3. Its mandate includes the conservation of rare manuscripts and conducting workshops on archival management.

How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation:
Statement 1 is correct: IGNCA is an autonomous institute under the Ministry of Culture.
Statement 2 is incorrect: It was established in 1985, though it was later designated as the Nodal Centre for India's engagement with the UNESCO Memory of the World programme in 2014.
Statement 3 is correct: IGNCA facilitates nominations, conserves rare manuscripts, and conducts workshops on archival management.
questions.takeaway: IGNCA, established in 1985 under the Ministry of Culture, acts as the Nodal Centre for India's UNESCO Memory of the World engagement since 2014.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Premier government-funded arts organization in India. It is an autonomous institute under the Ministry of Culture, established in 1985. India’s engagement is led by the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA), designated the Nodal Centre since 2014. IGNCA facilitates nominations, organizes awareness campaigns, conserves rare manuscripts, and conducts workshops on archival management.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt:
Consider the following pairs of Indian literary works inscribed in the UNESCO Memory of the World Asia-Pacific Regional Register and their key features:
1. Sahṛdayāloka-Locana : A text on Sanskrit poetics
2. Pañcatantra : A rare 15th-century manuscript
3. Rāmacaritamānasa : An illustrated manuscript

How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched. India's contributions to the Asia-Pacific Regional Register include the illustrated Rāmacaritamānasa, the Sahṛdayāloka-Locana (a Sanskrit poetics text), and a rare 15th-century Pañcatantra manuscript. These inscriptions were first made in 2023.
questions.takeaway: India's recent inscriptions to the Asia-Pacific Regional Register highlight its dedication to preserving storytelling, literary, and aesthetic traditions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India has also made significant contributions to the Asia-Pacific Regional Register. These include: The illustrated Rāmacaritamānasa; The Sahṛdayāloka-Locana (a Sanskrit poetics text); A rare 15th-century Pañcatantra manuscript. These inscriptions, first made in 2023, mark India’s growing regional participation...

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding India's engagement with the UNESCO Memory of the World Asia-Pacific Regional Register:
1. India made its first inscriptions to the Asia-Pacific Regional Register in the year 2023.
2. The nominations for the register from India are facilitated by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation:
Statement 1 is correct: India's first inscriptions to the Asia-Pacific Regional Register were made in 2023.
Statement 2 is incorrect: India's engagement, including facilitating nominations, is led by the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA), not the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
questions.takeaway: IGNCA is the nodal agency for India's UNESCO Memory of the World nominations, with the first regional inscriptions achieved in 2023.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India’s engagement is led by the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA), designated the Nodal Centre since 2014. IGNCA facilitates nominations... These inscriptions, first made in 2023, mark India’s growing regional participation...

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the functional scope of the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) in its capacity as the designated Nodal Centre for the UNESCO Memory of the World programme?

options.text: It serves as the primary regulatory body for the physical export of all historical artifacts from India.
It facilitates nominations, conducts archival management workshops, and conserves rare manuscripts.
It acts as a subsidiary of the Archaeological Survey of India for the excavation of ancient libraries.
It is the sole authority for the digital repatriation of Indian manuscripts held in foreign museums.

questions.explanation: As the Nodal Centre, IGNCA's mandate includes facilitating nominations for the UNESCO register, organizing awareness campaigns, conserving rare manuscripts, and conducting workshops on archival management.
questions.takeaway: The IGNCA's role as a Nodal Centre involves a combination of administrative (nominations), educational (workshops), and technical (conservation) functions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: IGNCA facilitates nominations, organizes awareness campaigns, conserves rare manuscripts, and conducts workshops on archival management.
